summary: Italian Mannerist painter from Florence. Most commonly identified by the Deposition from the Cross (Capponi Chapel) with its distinctive pale blue and pink figures. Also known for The Visitation, Vertumnus and Pomona lunette, and Joseph in Egypt. Student of Andrea del Sarto; teacher of Bronzino.

works.name: Deposition from the Cross (c. 1528)
works.description: Located above the altar in the Capponi Chapel (designed by Brunelleschi). Crowd of entangled figures in soft blue and pink cloaks surrounding the serpentine body of Christ. A crouching curly-haired figure supports himself on tiptoe. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Crowd of figures in pale blue and pink cloaks surrounding Christ's serpentine body; located in the Capponi Chapel

Christ's serpentine body winding through the billowing blue and pink cloaks

examples: located above the altarpiece of the Capponi Chapel

works.name: The Visitation
works.description: Depicts Elizabeth and Mary greeting each other, with two maids huddling around them. Bill Viola recreated it as a video piece. Composition copies Dürer's Four Witches.

clues.clue: Two maids huddle around Elizabeth and Mary; composition copies Dürer's Four Witches
examples: included two maids huddling around Elizabeth and Mary in his version of The Visitation
Pontormo's The Visitation copies the composition of its four figures from this artist's Four Witches

clues.clue: Bill Viola recreated it as a video where a pregnant woman whispers 'Can you help me?' to an older woman
examples: Bill Viola recreated a painting by this artist as a video piece in which a pregnant woman whispers, "Can you help me?" to an older woman

works.name: Vertumnus and Pomona (lunette)
works.description: Lunette at the Medici villa at Poggio a Caiano. Both title figures depicted as rustic peasants. Central oculus with putti.

clues.clue: Lunette at Medici villa at Poggio a Caiano; both figures as rustic peasants with sickle and fruit basket
examples: occupies a lunette in the Medici villa at Poggio a Caiano. Both of the title figures are depicted as rustic peasants

clues.clue: Adopted son / student Bronzino served as court painter to Cosimo de' Medici
examples: Pontormo's adopted son, Bronzino, painted this ruler's wife wearing an ornately-brocaded dress and served as his court painter

clues.clue: Vasari says he was tremendously afraid of death and avoided large gatherings; worked himself to death on a Last Judgement

recursive_suggestions: Mannerism — cross-topic with Parmigianino, El Greco, Bronzino, Rosso Fiorentino
comprehensive_summary:
Jacopo Pontormo was an Italian Mannerist painter from Florence. His most famous work is the Deposition from the Cross (c. 1528) in the Capponi Chapel (designed by Brunelleschi), depicting a crowd of entangled figures in soft blue and pink cloaks surrounding Christ's serpentine body. A crouching curly-haired figure in the foreground supports himself on tiptoe. The chapel also contains his Annunciation wall fresco with Mary in a jubilant contrapposto pose.

His Visitation includes two maids huddling around Elizabeth and Mary, with a composition that copies Dürer's Four Witches. Bill Viola recreated it as a video piece. The Vertumnus and Pomona lunette at the Medici villa at Poggio a Caiano shows both figures as rustic peasants with a sickle and fruit basket.

Pontormo's adopted son Bronzino served as court painter to Cosimo de' Medici. According to Vasari, Pontormo was tremendously afraid of death and avoided large gatherings, eventually working himself to death on a Last Judgement. His Joseph in Egypt series shows a child and father ascending a spiral staircase. His Portrait of a Halberdier is claimed to depict young Cosimo de' Medici.
